orderValleyQuestThree.questEleven: Select: Do you know when the movie starts?
orderValleyQuestThree.questElevenTip: The question word 'wann' in the middle of a sentence turns it into a subordinate clause and sends the verb to the end.
orderValleyQuestThree.questTwelve: Assemble: Do you know why she didn't come to school today?
orderValleyQuestThree.questTwelveTip: In Perfekt, the auxiliary verb stands after the participle.
orderValleyQuestThree.questThirteenTip: The conjunction 'dass' forces the verb to stand in the very last position, even after time-related words (morgen).
orderValleyQuestThree.questFourteen: Select: Tell me when you will come home.
orderValleyQuestThree.questFourteenTip: The question word 'wann' in the middle of a sentence works like a conjunction and requires the verb in the last position.
orderValleyQuestThree.questFifteen: Assemble: We are glad that you are learning German on skillupgerman.
orderValleyQuestThree.questFifteenTip: Even if a website name or other additions appear in the sentence, the conjunction 'dass' still pushes the conjugated verb to the very end.

orderValleyQuestFour.title: The Magic of 'Es gibt'
orderValleyQuestFour.description: Practicing the fixed expression 'es gibt' (there is/are). This link never changes, and whatever follows is always placed in Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestFour.goal: Practice Akkusativ after 'es gibt' and reinforce the fixed form of 'gibt'.
orderValleyQuestFour.questOne: Assemble: There is a park here.
orderValleyQuestFour.questOneTip: The construction 'es gibt' always requires Akkusativ. Masculine: ein -> einen.
orderValleyQuestFour.questTwo: Select: There is a museum in the city.
orderValleyQuestFour.questTwoTip: Museum is neuter (das); the article does not change in Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestFour.questThreeTip: In a question, the verb 'Gibt' comes first.
orderValleyQuestFour.questFour: Assemble: There are no problems here.
orderValleyQuestFour.questFourTip: Even if there are many items (Probleme), the verb 'gibt' remains singular.
orderValleyQuestFour.questFiveTip: If the sentence doesn't start with 'Es', the verb still stays in 2nd place, and 'es' moves to 3rd.
orderValleyQuestFour.questSix: Assemble: Is there a restaurant here?
orderValleyQuestFour.questSixTip: For a question, place 'Gibt' in the first position.
orderValleyQuestFour.questSevenTip: Fehler (mistake) is masculine, so use 'einen' in Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestFour.questEight: Select: There is milk in the fridge.
orderValleyQuestFour.questEightTip: The verb 'gibt' never turns into 'geben', even with abstract or uncountable nouns.
orderValleyQuestFour.questNine: Assemble: There is coffee for breakfast.
orderValleyQuestFour.questNineTip: Akkusativ for masculine (der Kaffee) is used without an article here, but the verb is in place.
orderValleyQuestFour.questTenTip: Standard question 'What is there today (for lunch/on the menu)?'
orderValleyQuestFour.questEleven: Select: There are many trees in the forest.
orderValleyQuestFour.questElevenTip: Plural does not affect the verb: it remains 'es gibt'.
orderValleyQuestFour.questTwelveTip: Use 'Es gibt' when saying that something exists in principle or is available.
orderValleyQuestFour.questThirteenTip: The phrase 'There is nothing / Nothing is available'.
orderValleyQuestFour.questFourteen: Select: Is there a supermarket here?
orderValleyQuestFour.questFourteenTip: Don't forget the Akkusativ for masculine gender.
orderValleyQuestFour.questFifteen: Assemble: There are many students in our school.
orderValleyQuestFour.questFifteenTip: (plural), but the verb 'gibt' is strictly singular.

orderValleyQuestFive.title: Es gibt Continued
orderValleyQuestFive.description: Practicing the 'es gibt' construction. Remember: this link never changes, and everything following it is always in Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestFive.goal: Automate the use of Akkusativ after 'es gibt'.
orderValleyQuestFive.questOne: Assemble the sentence: Is there an exit here?
orderValleyQuestFive.questOneTip: In a question, the order swaps. Remember Akkusativ for masculine.
orderValleyQuestFive.questTwo: Select the correct option: There is no entrance here.
orderValleyQuestFive.questFour: Assemble: There is a mistake here.
orderValleyQuestFive.questFourTip: Masculine (Fehler) gets the article 'einen' in Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestFive.questFive: Select: Where is there a toilet here?
orderValleyQuestFive.questFiveTip: After the question word 'Wo', the verb 'gibt' takes the second position.
orderValleyQuestFive.questSix: Assemble: There is a good reason.
orderValleyQuestFive.questSixTip: Grund is masculine. The article changes to 'einen' in Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestFive.questSevenTip: In a question, 'Gibt' stands before 'es'. The verb doesn't change even if there are many questions.
orderValleyQuestFive.questEight: Select: There are no classes today.
orderValleyQuestFive.questEightTip: Unterricht is masculine. Use the negation 'keinen' in Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestFive.questNine: Assemble: There is nothing more for today.
orderValleyQuestFive.questNineTip: The negation 'nichts' (nothing) is not declined and is simply placed after the verb.
orderValleyQuestFive.questTenTip: Lösung (solution) is feminine; the article 'eine' does not change.
orderValleyQuestFive.questEleven: Select: Is there a pharmacy nearby?
orderValleyQuestFive.questElevenTip: For a question, put the verb 'Gibt' first.
orderValleyQuestFive.questTwelve: Assemble: There is no hope for this.
orderValleyQuestFive.questTwelveTip: After 'es gibt', it's always Akkusativ. For feminine (Hoffnung), 'keine' looks the same as Nominativ.
orderValleyQuestFive.questThirteenTip: Fixed expression: 'There is a lot to do'.
orderValleyQuestFive.questFourteen: Select: There is a letter in this bag.
orderValleyQuestFive.questFourteenTip: The construction 'es gibt' always requires Akkusativ, so the article changes accordingly.
orderValleyQuestFive.questFifteen: Assemble: There is one more chance.
orderValleyQuestFive.questFifteenTip: Chance is feminine. 'Es gibt' always requires Akkusativ.

orderValleyQuestSix.title: Reflexive Pronouns
orderValleyQuestSix.description: Distinguishing between Akkusativ and Dativ. If the action is directed toward an object (hair, teeth, hands), use the Dativ form.
orderValleyQuestSix.goal: Understand the difference between Akkusativ and Dativ in reflexive verbs.
orderValleyQuestSix.questOneTip: There is no object here, the action is simply on oneself. Use Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestSix.questTwo: Select: You are combing your hair.
orderValleyQuestSix.questTwoTip: There is an object, so the reflexive pronoun is placed in Dativ.
orderValleyQuestSix.questThreeTip: In the 3rd person, the form 'sich' is always the same for both cases.
orderValleyQuestSix.questFour: Assemble: We are brushing our teeth.
orderValleyQuestSix.questFourTip: There is an object here. For 'wir', the form is the same in both cases.
orderValleyQuestSix.questSix: Assemble: I am washing my hands.
orderValleyQuestSix.questSixTip: There is an object, so it is Dativ.
orderValleyQuestSix.questSevenTip: Please take a seat. The polite form 'Sie' requires 'sich'.
orderValleyQuestSix.questEight: Select: I am ordering myself a pizza.
orderValleyQuestSix.questEightTip: Pizza is a direct object. Use Dativ.
orderValleyQuestSix.questNineTip: No object, action is on oneself. Akkusativ. Don't forget the separable prefix.
orderValleyQuestSix.questTen: Select: You are putting on a jacket.
orderValleyQuestSix.questTenTip: There is an object here. So it is Dativ.
orderValleyQuestSix.questElevenTip: We are meeting. For 'wir', the form does not change in either case.
orderValleyQuestSix.questTwelveTip: In this case, 'sich merken' (to memorize/remember) always requires Dativ.
orderValleyQuestSix.questFourteen: Assemble: You are watching a video (plural).
orderValleyQuestSix.questFourteenTip: To watch something - 'sich anschauen': requires Dativ.
orderValleyQuestSix.questFifteenTip: Hurt (whom? oneself). Akkusativ for the neuter gender.

orderValleyQuestSeven.title: Everything at Once
orderValleyQuestSeven.description: In every sentence, you must track word order in the subordinate clause, cases after 'es gibt', and reflexive pronouns.
orderValleyQuestSeven.goal: Consolidate all complex topics in a mixed mode.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questOne: Assemble: I know that there is a mistake here.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questOneTip: 'dass' throws the verb to the end, and 'es gibt' requires Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questTwo: Select: He says that he is washing himself now.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questTwoTip: In a subordinate clause, the reflexive 'sich' stands immediately after the subject.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questThreeTip: Ausgang (exit) is masculine. After 'es gibt' in a subordinate clause, use Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questFour: Assemble: I see that you are combing your hair.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questFourTip: There is an object, so we use Dativ. The verb goes to the end.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questFive: Select: I am in a hurry because time is short.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questFiveTip: The verb 'beeilen' requires Akkusativ. At the end is the 'es gibt' construction.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questSix: Assemble: I will remember that there is a mistake here.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questSixTip: 'Sich merken' is Dativ. In the subordinate clause, 'es gibt' requires Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questEight: Select: We are happy that there is a solution.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questEightTip: The conjunction 'dass' sends the verb to the end, and the phrase 'es gibt' requires Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questNineTip: Reflexive verb in a subordinate clause: 'sich' before the verb.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questTen: Select: I will order myself a pizza if there is a discount.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questTenTip: Ordering for oneself is Dativ. After 'wenn', the verb is at the very end.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questEleven: Select: You see that there is a problem here.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questElevenTip: The conjunction 'dass' sends the verb to the very end, and 'es gibt' requires Akkusativ after it.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questTwelve: Assemble: It is important that there is an entrance here.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questTwelveTip: In a subordinate clause after 'dass', the verb goes to the last place.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questThirteen: Select: I am happy because there is a discount.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questThirteenTip: To be happy - 'sich freuen' (Akkusativ). After 'es gibt', the verb goes to the end.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questFourteen: Assemble: I will rent myself an apartment if there is a balcony.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questFourteenTip: There is an object, so it's Dativ (mir). After 'es gibt', it gets Akkusativ. Verb at the end because of 'wenn'.
orderValleyQuestSeven.questFifteenTip: Subordinate clause - verb at the end.

orderValleyQuestEight.title: Travel
orderValleyQuestEight.description: Practicing everything at once regarding holidays: subordinate clauses, 'es gibt', and reflexive verbs in a travel context.
orderValleyQuestEight.goal: Consolidate word order and cases using travel examples.
orderValleyQuestEight.questOne: Assemble: I know that there is a beach here.
orderValleyQuestEight.questTwo: Select: He says that he is resting now.
orderValleyQuestEight.questTwoTip: Sich ausruhen is a reflexive verb. In a subordinate clause, 'sich' follows the subject.
orderValleyQuestEight.questThreeTip: In a subordinate clause, 'gibt' stands at the very end.
orderValleyQuestEight.questFour: Assemble: He orders himself a taxi because there is no subway here.
orderValleyQuestEight.questFourTip: Ordering for oneself is Dativ. After 'da' (since), the verb goes to the end.
orderValleyQuestEight.questFive: Select: We are happy because there is a discount on the tour.
orderValleyQuestEight.questFiveTip: After 'weil', the verb goes to the end.
orderValleyQuestEight.questSix: Assemble: I will remember that there is a station here.
orderValleyQuestEight.questSixTip: Sich merken — Dativ. Use the indefinite article in Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestEight.questSevenTip: Airport (Flughafen) is masculine. The case after 'es gibt' is Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestEight.questEight: Select: I'm angry that there is no space.
orderValleyQuestEight.questEightTip: After 'es gibt', Akkusativ is required, and the verb goes to the end.
orderValleyQuestEight.questNine: Assemble: I am booking myself a room if there is a vacancy.
orderValleyQuestEight.questNineTip: Booking for oneself — Dativ. + indefinite article in Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestEight.questTen: Select: You see that there is a cafe here.
orderValleyQuestEight.questTenTip: Café is neuter. After 'dass', the verb is at the very end.
orderValleyQuestEight.questElevenTip: Looking forward to something (future) — sich auf etwas freuen.
orderValleyQuestEight.questTwelve: Assemble: It's important that there is a pool here.
orderValleyQuestEight.questTwelveTip: Verb 'gibt' in the last place. + indefinite article in Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestEight.questThirteen: Select: Happy that there is an excursion.
orderValleyQuestEight.questThirteenTip: To be happy — 'sich freuen'. Indefinite article in Akkusativ. Verb at the end.
orderValleyQuestEight.questFourteen: Assemble: You take a car for yourself if there is a rental here.
orderValleyQuestEight.questFourteenTip: If the question is 'To whom?' (Wem?), use Dativ. 'Wenn' sends the verb to the end.
orderValleyQuestEight.questFifteenTip: The conjunction 'dass' moves the verb to the end, and 'es gibt' requires Akkusativ. Since it's plural, the negation ends in -e.

orderValleyQuestNine.title: Work
orderValleyQuestNine.description: Interviews, deadlines, and job hunting. Practicing word order and reflexive pronouns.
orderValleyQuestNine.goal: Consolidate topics in the context of career and office.
orderValleyQuestNine.questOne: Assemble: I will apply (for the job) if there is work.
orderValleyQuestNine.questOneTip: The verb 'sich bewerben' requires Akkusativ. The conjunction 'wenn' sends the verb to the end.
orderValleyQuestNine.questTwo: Select: He says that he is introducing himself now.
orderValleyQuestNine.questTwoTip: Sich vorstellen — to introduce oneself. In a 'dass' clause, the conjugated verb is last.
orderValleyQuestNine.questThreeTip: After 'dass', the verb goes to the end, and 'es gibt' requires Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestNine.questFour: Assemble: I will remember that there is a meeting.
orderValleyQuestNine.questFourTip: Sich merken — to remember (Dativ). In the subordinate clause, 'es gibt' requires Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestNine.questFive: Select: I'm glad because there is a bonus.
orderValleyQuestNine.questFiveTip: Bonus is masculine. After 'weil', the verb 'gibt' goes to the very end.
orderValleyQuestNine.questSix: Assemble: I'm looking for a job for myself if there is a vacancy.
orderValleyQuestNine.questSixTip: When doing something for oneself (looking for, buying), use Dativ (mir). After 'wenn', the verb is last.
orderValleyQuestNine.questSevenTip: The conjunction 'ob' (whether) turns it into a subordinate clause. 'Es gibt' always requires Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestNine.questEight: Select: We are angry that there is no work.
orderValleyQuestNine.questEightTip: To be angry — sich ärgern. In the subordinate clause, 'dass' sends the verb to the last place.
orderValleyQuestNine.questNine: Assemble: I'll buy myself a laptop if there is a discount.
orderValleyQuestNine.questNineTip: For whom (myself) is Dativ (mir). After 'es gibt', the object is Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestNine.questTen: Select: You see that there is a problem.
orderValleyQuestNine.questTenTip: Problem is neuter. 'Dass' sends the verb to the end; 'es gibt' requires Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestNine.questElevenTip: Sich vorbereiten — to prepare. In the subordinate clause, 'sich' follows the subject.
orderValleyQuestNine.questTwelve: Assemble: It's important that there is a plan.
orderValleyQuestNine.questTwelveTip: Plan is masculine (einen Plan). After 'dass', the verb goes to the end.
orderValleyQuestNine.questThirteen: Select: I'm in a hurry because there is a deadline.
orderValleyQuestNine.questThirteenTip: Sich beeilen (to hurry) — Akkusativ. 'Termin' often means a deadline or appointment.
orderValleyQuestNine.questFourteen: Assemble: I'll take this job if there is a contract.
orderValleyQuestNine.questFourteenTip: Sich nehmen (to take for oneself) — Dativ. Verb at the end due to 'wenn'.
orderValleyQuestNine.questFifteenTip: Geld is neuter. The negation 'kein' in Akkusativ does not change form for neuter.

orderValleyQuestTen.title: Work: Simple and Clear
orderValleyQuestTen.description: Practicing word order and reflexive verbs on simple topics: illness, breaks, being late, and money.
orderValleyQuestTen.goal: Practice grammar with the simplest examples.
orderValleyQuestTen.questOne: Assemble: I am not working today because I am ill.
orderValleyQuestTen.questOneTip: The conjunction 'weil' sends the verb to the end of the sentence.
orderValleyQuestTen.questTwo: Select: He says he is late.
orderValleyQuestTen.questTwoTip: To be late — 'sich verspäten'. In the subordinate clause, the verb moves to the end.
orderValleyQuestTen.questFour: Assemble: I am happy that I have a day off.
orderValleyQuestTen.questFourTip: To be happy — 'sich freuen' (Akkusativ). The verb stands at the end after 'dass'.
orderValleyQuestTen.questFive: Select: I am in a hurry because there are things to do.
orderValleyQuestTen.questFiveTip: To hurry — 'sich beeilen'. In the subordinate clause, 'weil' sends the verb to the end.
orderValleyQuestTen.questSix: Assemble: I will remember that there is a meeting tomorrow.
orderValleyQuestTen.questSixTip: For whom (to oneself) — this is Dative, and after the verb and 'es gibt', it is always (what?) — Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestTen.questEight: Select: We are angry because there is no break.
orderValleyQuestTen.questEightTip: To be angry — 'sich ärgern'. Indefinite article in Akk.
orderValleyQuestTen.questNine: Assemble: I will buy it if there is money.
orderValleyQuestTen.questNineTip: Buying for oneself — Dative. The verb 'gibt' moves to the end.
orderValleyQuestTen.questTen: Select: You see that there is a problem.
orderValleyQuestTen.questTenTip: After 'es gibt', it's always Akkusativ, but only the masculine gender gets the -en ending (einen). 'Problem' is neuter (das), so the article does not change (ein).
orderValleyQuestTen.questElevenTip: To wash oneself — 'sich waschen'. In the subordinate clause, 'dass' sends the verb to the end.
orderValleyQuestTen.questTwelve: Assemble: It is important that there is work.
orderValleyQuestTen.questTwelveTip: In the subordinate clause (dass), the verb 'gibt' stands in the last place.
orderValleyQuestTen.questThirteen: Select: I am in a hurry because time is short.
orderValleyQuestTen.questThirteenTip: Sich beeilen (to hurry) requires Akkusativ. After 'weil', the verb 'gibt' always moves to the end.
orderValleyQuestTen.questFourteen: Assemble: I will take it if there is a receipt.
orderValleyQuestTen.questFourteenTip: Taking for oneself — Dative. After 'wenn', the verb 'gibt' moves to the end, preceded by the Akkusativ.
orderValleyQuestTen.questFifteenTip: After 'es gibt', it's always Akkusativ. For masculine gender (der Stress), the negation 'kein' must take the -en ending: 'keinen'.

orderValleyQuestEleven.title: Grammar: New Level
orderValleyQuestEleven.description: Practicing word order, reflexive verbs, and 'es gibt' in various daily situations: food, rest, shopping, and plans.
orderValleyQuestEleven.goal: Consolidate topics with fresh examples without repetition.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questOne: Assemble: I'll order myself a pizza because there's no pasta.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questOneTip: For whom (oneself) — Dative; what (is/exists) — always Akkusativ. Verb 'gibt' goes to the end due to 'weil'.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questTwo: Select: He asks if there is an entrance here.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questTwoTip: Recipient (oneself) — Dative; object — Akkusativ. Verb 'gibt' moves to the end due to 'ob' (if).
orderValleyQuestEleven.questThree: Write: I rest when there is time.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questThreeTip: Recipient — Dative; object — Akkusativ. Verb 'gibt' moves to the end due to 'wenn'.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questFour: Assemble: She says she is putting on makeup now.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questFourTip: Recipient (oneself) — Dative (mir). In a 'dass' clause, the reflexive particle (sich) is placed immediately after the subject.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questFive: Select: I'll buy myself a jacket if there's a discount.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questFiveTip: Recipient — Dative; object — Akkusativ. Verb 'gibt' moves to the end due to 'wenn'.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questSix: Assemble: It's a pity that there is no towel.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questSixTip: Recipient — Dative; object — Akkusativ. Verb 'gibt' moves to the end due to 'dass'.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questSevenTip: Recipient — Dative; object — Akkusativ. Verb 'gibt' moves to the end due to 'weil'.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questEight: Select: We are angry that there is no sun.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questEightTip: Recipient — Dative; object — Akkusativ. Verb 'gibt' moves to the end due to 'dass'.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questNine: Assemble: I'll check the car if there's a key.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questNineTip: Recipient — Dative; object — Akkusativ. Verb 'gibt' moves to the end due to 'wenn'.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questTen: Select: You see that there is beer.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questTenTip: Recipient — Dative; object — Akkusativ. Verb 'gibt' moves to the end due to 'dass'.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questElevenTip: In a 'dass' clause, the reflexive particle (sich) is placed immediately after the subject.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questTwelve: Assemble: It is important that there is a coach.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questTwelveTip: Recipient — Dative; object — Akkusativ. Verb 'gibt' moves to the end due to 'dass'.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questThirteen: Select: I am in a hurry because there is an appointment.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questThirteenTip: Recipient — Dative; object — Akkusativ. Verb 'gibt' moves to the end due to 'weil'.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questFourteen: Assemble: I'll get myself a coffee if there's milk.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questFourteenTip: Recipient — Dative; object — Akkusativ. Verb 'gibt' moves to the end due to 'wenn'.
orderValleyQuestEleven.questFifteenTip: Recipient — Dative; object — Akkusativ. Verb 'gibt' moves to the end due to 'dass'.

orderValleyQuestTwelve.title: Home and Leisure: Grammar
orderValleyQuestTwelve.description: Practicing word order, reflexive verbs, and 'es gibt' in everyday situations: apartment, friends, hobbies, and relaxation.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.goal: Consolidate rules on the topic of daily life.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questOne: Assemble: I'm going to the meeting because there's a party there.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questOneTip: Recipient — Dative; object — Akkusativ. Verb 'gibt' goes to the end due to 'weil'.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questTwo: Select: He says he is watching a movie.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questTwoTip: Recipient — Dative (mir); object — Akkusativ. In a 'dass' clause, the verb moves to the end.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questThreeTip: Recipient — Dative; object — Akkusativ. Verb 'gibt' moves to the end due to 'dass'.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questFour: Assemble: I will think about it if there is a chance.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questFourTip: Recipient — Dative; object — Akkusativ. Verb 'gibt' moves to the end due to 'wenn'.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questFive: Select: I am putting on a white shirt because I am going on a date.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questFiveTip: When naming a specific item (shirt — Hemd), we use Dative (mir). 'Weil' sends the verb to the end.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questSix: Assemble: It's a pity there's no dog here.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questSixTip: After 'es gibt', it's always Akkusativ. For masculine gender (der Hund), 'kein' must become 'keinen'.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questSevenTip: The conjunction 'ob' (if) sends the verb 'gibt' to the end. 'Balkon' is in Akkusativ, so use 'einen'.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questEight: Select: We are resting because there is no stress.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questEightTip: Reflexive 'uns' for 'we rest'. In 'es gibt', negation is 'kein'. 'Weil' sends 'gibt' to the end.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questNine: Assemble: I'll look at the apartment if there's a key.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questNineTip: In 'sich etwas anschauen', the pronoun is Dative (mir). 'Wenn' sends 'gibt' to the end.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questTen: Select: You see there's a mistake there.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questTenTip: 'Dass' puts 'gibt' at the end. Remember Akkusativ: 'der Fehler' becomes 'einen Fehler'.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questElevenTip: The conjunction 'dass' puts 'wäscht' at the end. Since he washes 'himself' entirely, use Akkusativ — 'sich'.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questTwelve: Assemble: It's important that there is a garden.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questTwelveTip: 'Es gibt' requires Akkusativ. 'Dass' sends 'gibt' to the very end.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questThirteen: Select: I am washing my hands because there is soap.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questThirteenTip: When washing a specific body part (hands), use 'mir' (Dative). 'Weil' sends 'gibt' to the end.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questFourteen: Assemble: I will take the room if there is a bed.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questFourteenTip: In 'sich (Dative) etwas nehmen', we use 'mir'. After 'es gibt' comes Akkusativ. 'Gibt' goes to the end after 'wenn'.
orderValleyQuestTwelve.questFifteenTip: Negation with 'es gibt' is 'kein'. After 'dass', the verb 'gibt' always escapes to the very end.

footer.copy: Der Die Das. All rights reserved.

regions.descriptionEastPlain: Expansive lands where everyone begins their journey. Here you will master the basics of the language and receive your first quests. From here the great adventures begin.
regions.descriptionStoneReach: A harsh land with stony roads. Here you will strengthen your skills on the path to mastery.
regions.descriptionWindValley: The land of eternal storms and changeable weather. Gusts of wind bring trials, but along with them new knowledge. Here you face true challenges on the path of language learning.
regions.descriptionLakeRuins: Mist envelops ancient structures, and the waters of the lakes hold secrets of the past. In these lands lie forgotten knowledge that will help you delve deeper into language learning.
regions.descriptionNorthCliffs: Tall cliffs rise above the sea, and cold winds test the endurance of every traveler. Tasks requiring attentiveness await you.
regions.descriptionStormCoast: Wild coastal lands where thunder and lightning rage. Only brave heroes dare to set foot here. Here you face a true test of the power of your knowledge.
regions.descriptionMasterLands: Lands of masters and craftsmen, where every labor matters. Here you will learn how to talk about professions, work, and daily duties.
regions.descriptionWildLands: Nature is full of amazing discoveries. Here you will learn how to name forest inhabitants and elements, describe their actions, and form sentences about the natural world.
regions.descriptionPluralLands: On this steep slope, the mist of the past finally clears, revealing a view of the distant horizon. Here, the traveler learns not just to look at their feet, but to peer into both tomorrow and the days gone by.
regions.descriptionPastLands: Among the ancient stones and sands of time, stories of what has already come to pass are kept. Here, the traveler learns to look back and describe the events of yesteryear, mastering the Past Tense.
regions.descriptionActionLands: Here, time no longer stands still, and the mist of the past dissipates. In this valley, the traveler learns to set goals, mastering modal verbs and the art of interaction through grammatical cases.
regions.descriptionOrderValley: Here, the traveler learns to build proper sentences. In this valley, they master word order in simple and complex phrases, learn to report the presence of things, and use reflexive pronouns.
regions.descriptionTimeGate: Here, at the time gate, the traveler gains confidence, looking back and peering forward. In this section, we will review: the traveler revisits and consolidates the past and future tenses.
regions.descriptionEdgeImages: Working with definite articles in the past and future tenses. The traveler learns to identify correctly and takes the necessary form from the article in each sentence.
regions.descriptionSecretLands: Working with indefinite articles in the past and future tenses. The traveler learns to identify correctly and takes the necessary form from the article in each sentence.
regions.descriptionDefinite: Working with both definite and indefinite articles in the past and future tenses. Learning to distinguish between them and correctly place the necessary form from the article in each sentence.

metaAdjectiveBasic.title: Skillupgerman — Adjectives A1–A2: colors, feelings, appearance, character, size
metaAdjectiveBasic.description: Learn basic German adjectives by topics: colors, feelings and emotions, appearance, character, size and shape. Examples with translation and audio + trainers.

metaAdjectiveComparison.title: Degrees of comparison of adjectives in German: Komparativ and Superlativ rules, umlaut, special forms
metaAdjectiveComparison.description: How to form degrees of comparison of adjectives in German: rules for forms with -er and -sten, adjectives with umlaut (ä/ö/ü), exceptions and irregular forms. Examples with translation, audio and exercises.

metaAdjectiveDeclension.title: Declension of adjectives in German after definite/indefinite article and without article
metaAdjectiveDeclension.description: Rules and tables of adjective endings in German: after the definite article (der/die/das), after the indefinite (ein/kein/mein) and without an article. Examples, translation, practice.

metaArticle.title: Training German articles der, die, das in game form
metaArticle.description: Learn German articles der, die, das easily and with fun! Choose a topic and practice in five interactive modes: memorization, writing, plural, audio and word building.

metaTests.title: German tests: A1, A2, B1, B2 (Lesen, Hören, Schreiben, Sprechen)
metaTests.description: Practice German tests for levels A1–B2: reading, listening, writing and speaking. Instant check and result saving. Test your knowledge.

metaLocation.title: Skillupgerman — Language Lands for learning German
metaLocation.description: Explore language lands and level up your German by completing tasks and passing levels. Learn vocabulary and grammar in adventure format with quests and progression, like in a game!

metaModalVerbs.title: Modal verbs and subordinate clauses (Nebensätze) in German
metaModalVerbs.description: Learn modal verbs (können, müssen, wollen, mögen) and subordinate clauses (Nebensätze: weil, dass, wenn) in German. Examples, translation and practice.

metaPrepositions.title: German prepositions by cases: Nominativ, Akkusativ, Dativ, Genitiv
metaPrepositions.description: Learn German prepositions by cases: Nominativ, Akkusativ, Dativ and Genitiv. Simple rules, grammar, examples and mini-quiz for practice.

metaTenses.title: German tenses: Präsens, Perfekt, Präteritum, Futur I, Plusquamperfekt
metaTenses.description: Clear and simple explanations of German tenses with examples and tables: Präsens, Perfekt, Präteritum, Futur I and Plusquamperfekt. Start practicing each tense.

metaThematic.title: Thematic levels: insert der, die, das in the right case
metaThematic.description: Choose a topic and go through difficulty levels: practice articles der, die, das in Nominativ, Akkusativ, Dativ and Genitiv. Short sentences, clear progression, practice.

metaVerbTypes.title: Types of German verbs: with prepositions, separable prefixes, strong and reflexive
metaVerbTypes.description: Types of verbs in German: verbs with fixed prepositions (auf, an, mit, von etc.), with separable prefixes (anrufen, ankommen), irregular/strong (fahren, helfen, sprechen) and reflexive. Examples, mini-rules, theory and practice.

metaAdjectiveTheory.title: German adjectives: endings, declension, comparison
metaAdjectiveTheory.description: Overview of adjectives in German: when endings change, weak/strong/mixed declension, tables by cases, Komparativ and Superlativ, common mistakes + mini-quiz.

metaArticles.title: Der / Die / Das — introduction. What are articles
metaArticles.description: Short and clear: when to use der, die, das; definite and indefinite articles, gender table.

metaPrepositionsTheory.title: German prepositions by cases with examples and test
metaPrepositionsTheory.description: Learn German prepositions by cases: Dativ, Akkusativ, Genitiv and ‘chameleons’. Sentence examples, contractions (am, im, ins) and mini-quiz for practice.

metaIdioms.title: German idioms and phrases with examples and translation
metaIdioms.description: Learn German idioms and phrases: fixed expressions, figurative meanings and real-life examples. With translation and explanations for beginners and advanced learners + mini-quiz.

metaMainPage.title: Learn German in game form
metaMainPage.description: Skillupgerman - game-like training of articles der, die, das, thematic levels, verbs, adjectives, prepositions, quests and achievements. Learn German with fun!

metaVerbsTheory.title: Theory of German verbs with examples and quiz
metaVerbsTheory.description: Overview of conjugation, tenses and auxiliary verbs in German: machen/lernen, haben/sein, Futur, word order. Examples, tables and mini-quiz.

howToUse.title: How to use
howToUse.answerOne: To start practicing German, simply register and create an account. Without this, access to tasks will be closed.
howToUse.answerTwo: Seasonal events are temporary activities tied to holidays. They include special quests, themed tasks, and unique rewards available only for a limited time.
howToUse.answerThree: An achievement is a mark for a completed activity (progress, correct answers, daily tasks, etc.).
howToUse.answerFour: Rewards are given for completing certain achievements and are displayed in your profile.
howToUse.questFive: Where to check progress on articles?
howToUse.answerFive: On the main page in the 'Participant Panel' select 'Statistics', then — the progress of the desired category. Important: if you don’t have active topics on articles, statistics will not be displayed.
howToUse.answerSix: Artiklus is the internal currency. It is earned for correct answers in the 'Article Practice' task and for completing tasks in Language Lands. It can be spent on extra attempts.
howToUse.questSeven: How to get into the ranking?
howToUse.answerSeven: You need to reach at least level 2 by playing 'Article Practice' or 'Language Lands'. After that, the ranking table becomes available.
howToUse.answerEight: These are tasks by topic that can be completed once a day. You can complete 3 tasks per day. They are available on the main page.
howToUse.answerNine: These are temporary events that take place once a year. During the active period, themed quests and rewards are available. Outside the date range, the section is inaccessible. The event dates can be found in the 'Event Calendar' in the Participant Panel.

accountManagement.title: Account Management
accountManagement.questOne: How to change name or email?
accountManagement.questAnswer: At the moment changing name or email is not supported.
accountManagement.answerTwo: Profile → 'Account settings' tab → 'Delete account'. After deletion all your data will be permanently removed and cannot be restored.
accountManagement.answerThree: On the login form select 'Forgot password?' and follow the instructions. A recovery email will be sent to the address provided during registration. Important: use a real email address when registering.

subscribe.title: Subscription (Premium)
subscribe.questOneStepOne: 1 Audio for all words and sentences — practice listening comprehension.
subscribe.questOneStepTwo: 2 Unlimited learning — go through as much material as you want per day.
subscribe.questOneStepThree: 3 Access to all tests with auto-check and AI evaluation.
subscribe.questOneStepFour: 4 Audio tasks and speaking tasks for oral practice.
subscribe.questOneStepFive: 5 Exclusive materials and exercises not available in the free version.
subscribe.questOneStepSix: 6 Support developers — Premium helps us improve the project, release new tasks and enhance features for all students. Our goal is to make learning German accessible and fun. We create an atmosphere of interactivity, games and competition so that studying feels like an adventure you want to return to every day!
subscribe.note: After this, access remains until the end of the paid period.
subscribe.questThree: Is there a refund for unused days?
subscribe.answerThree: Currently refunds for unused days are not available.

duelGrammarTips.authText: To play duels, please log in or register.
duelGrammarTips.tipOne: Verb goes in 2nd position: Ich **lerne** Deutsch.
duelGrammarTips.tipTwo: Place or time at the beginning? Verb is still 2nd: **Heute** gehe ich.
duelGrammarTips.tipThree: Yes/No question: verb in 1st place — **Kommst** du?
duelGrammarTips.tipFour: Subordinate clause: verb at the end — …weil ich **arbeite**.
duelGrammarTips.tipFive: Conjunction **und** does not change word order: Ich lerne und ich **spiele**.

examIndexPage.choice: Choose a level and start practicing all modules:

nominativ.rule: There are almost no special prepositions for the nominative. This case is usually used for the subject and after the verbs sein / werden / bleiben.
nominativ.tipOne: Nominative answers the questions Wer? / Was?
nominativ.tipTwo: After sein, werden, bleiben — the predicate noun is in the nominative.
nominativ.tipThree: If there is a preposition, it is almost never nominative.
nominativ.practiceDescription: Identify the uses of nominative without prepositions.

akkusativ.rule: These prepositions always require the accusative: durch, für, gegen, ohne, um, bis, and also entlang (often as a postposition).
akkusativ.tipOne: Hack: FÜR, UM, DURCH, GEGEN, OHNE, (BIS), ENTLANG.
akkusativ.tipTwo: Movement 'to where' is often expressed with the accusative.
akkusativ.tipThree: bis is usually without article: bis Montag, bis Berlin.
akkusativ.tipFour: entlang — as a postposition: den Fluss entlang.
akkusativ.translateOne: We are walking through the park.
akkusativ.practiceDescription: Practice the prepositions that require the accusative.

dativ.rule: These prepositions always require the dative: aus, außer, bei, mit, nach, seit, von, zu, gegenüber.
dativ.tipOne: Basic set: bei, mit, nach, seit, von, zu.
dativ.tipTwo: The state 'where?' is often expressed with the dative.
dativ.tipThree: gegenüber can stand after the noun: dem Haus gegenüber.
dativ.translateOne: He comes out of the house.
dativ.translateTwo: There was no one except the child.
dativ.translateSix: I have been learning German for a year.
dativ.translateSeven: The train leaves from the station.
dativ.translateNine: Opposite the house is a park.
dativ.practiceDescription: Review the prepositions that require the dative.

genitiv.rule: These prepositions require the genitive: statt, trotz, während, wegen, außerhalb, innerhalb, jenseits/diesseits, aufgrund.
genitiv.tipOne: In spoken German, the genitive is often replaced by von + dative.
genitiv.tipTwo: Articles: des/eines; nouns often get the ending -s/-es.
genitiv.tipThree: Adjectives in the genitive get the ending -en.
genitiv.translateOne: Instead of sugar I take honey.
genitiv.translateTwo: Despite the rain, we go for a walk.
genitiv.translateThree: During the lesson the phone is off.
genitiv.translateFour: Because of the cold we stay at home.
genitiv.translateFive: Outside the city it is quieter.
genitiv.translateSix: Within a week you will get an answer.
genitiv.translateSeven: On the other side of the river is a village.
genitiv.translateEight: Due to roadworks the road is closed.
genitiv.practiceDescription: Practice the use of genitive prepositions.

adjectiveTheory.title: Adjectives in German: simple and clear
adjectiveTheory.subTitle: What they are, where they stand, how they are compared and which endings to use. At the end — mini quiz.
adjectiveTheory.question: Question

adjectiveTheoryFirstBlock.title: 1) What is an adjective?
adjectiveTheoryFirstBlock.subtitlePartTwo: (Adjektiv) — a word that answers the question 'what kind?'. Examples:
adjectiveTheoryFirstBlock.noteOnePartTwo: adjectives occur in two positions — this determines whether endings are needed.

adjectiveTheorySecondBlock.title: 2) How to choose an ending: super short algorithm
adjectiveTheorySecondBlock.exampleThreePartTwo: In Akkusativ, Dativ and Genitiv almost always ending
adjectiveTheorySecondBlock.tipPartTwo: if in doubt in oblique cases (Akk/Dat/Gen) — most often the ending

adjectiveTheoryThirdBlock.title: 3) Example: `ein` + adjective
adjectiveTheoryThirdBlock.paragraph: This is the most important topic for beginners. The article `ein` (one, some) does not always show the gender of the noun. In this case, the adjective takes over this function.
adjectiveTheoryThirdBlock.noteOnePartTwo: the adjective gets an ending that 'tells' us the gender of the noun, just like the definite article would do
adjectiveTheoryThirdBlock.exampleThreePartOne: The article `eine` already shows feminine, so the adjective only needs a simple ending
adjectiveTheoryThirdBlock.paragraphTwo: Let’s see how it works in sentences:
adjectiveTheoryThirdBlock.tableExampleOne: Examples in Nominativ (Who? What?) and Akkusativ (Whom? What?)
adjectiveTheoryThirdBlock.tipOnePartTwo: In Akkusativ masculine changes (`ein` → `einen`), and the adjective almost always takes ending
adjectiveTheoryThirdBlock.tipOnePartThree: Feminine and neuter in Akkusativ look the same as in Nominativ.

adjectiveTheoryFourthBlock.title: 4) Mini tables for start
adjectiveTheoryFourthBlock.type: Idea of weak declension: in Nominativ singular often

verbsTheory.title: Verbs in German
verbsTheory.subTitle: Basics for a confident start: Präsens, Perfekt, Präteritum and Futur I. We cover word order and common mistakes.

verbTheoryFirstBlock.title: 1) Word order: verb in 2nd position
verbTheoryFirstBlock.subTitleSecondPart: — is the change of a verb by persons and numbers (I do, you do...). In German it is the basis of a sentence.
verbTheoryFirstBlock.rulePartSecond: in a simple declarative sentence the conjugated verb
verbTheoryFirstBlock.rulePartThird: is always in the second position
verbTheoryFirstBlock.paragraphPartOne: If there is a second verb in the sentence (e.g. infinitive or Partizip II), it goes to the very

verbTheorySecondBlock.title: 2) Präsens — present tense
verbTheorySecondBlock.subTitle: The most common tense, used to describe actions in the present or future (with a time marker). Most verbs have the same endings.
verbTheorySecondBlock.paragraphPartTwo: In the 2nd and 3rd person singular (du, er/sie/es) the stem vowel often changes: f

verbTheoryThirdBlock.title: 3) Perfekt — spoken past tense
verbTheoryThirdBlock.subTitle: Used for completed actions, especially in spoken language. Formula:

verbTheoryFourthBlock.title: 4) Präteritum — 'book' past tense
verbTheoryFourthBlock.subTitlePartOne: This tense is mostly found in books, news and stories. In spoken language it is mainly used for

verbTheoryFifthBlock.title: 5) Futur I — future tense
verbTheoryFifthBlock.paragraph: Used to express plans or assumptions about the future. Formula:
verbTheoryFifthBlock.noteThreeSecondPart: in spoken German Präsens is often used for the future if the context makes it clear (tomorrow, the day after tomorrow):

verbTheorySixthBlock.title: 6) Typical mistakes
verbTheorySixthBlock.typeOnePartSecond: Conjugated verb not in the second position.
verbTheorySixthBlock.typeTwoPartTwo: Second part of the verb (in Perfekt, Futur I) not at the end.

verbTheorySeventhBlock.questOne: How to say correctly: "I am learning German"?
verbTheorySeventhBlock.questTwo: Choose the correct word order: "Today I am going to the cinema".
verbTheorySeventhBlock.questThree: How will it be in Perfekt: "We did a lot"?
verbTheorySeventhBlock.questFour: Perfekt with a verb of motion: "He went home".
verbTheorySeventhBlock.questFive: Präteritum of the verb "sein": "I was tired".
verbTheorySeventhBlock.questSeven: Choose the correct Partizip II of the verb "sehen" (to see).

masterLandsThirdQuests.title: Colleagues and Team
masterLandsThirdQuests.description: Learning to talk about colleagues and the team.

masterLandsThirdQuests.questSeven: Assemble the sentence: We work together
masterLandsThirdQuests.quesTen: How to say: I work alone
masterLandsThirdQuests.quesThirteen: Assemble the sentence: The boss is here

masterLandsFourthQuests.title: Working Hours and Schedule
masterLandsFourthQuests.description: In this task, we learn to talk about the beginning and end of the workday

masterLandsFourthQuests.questSix: Assemble the sentence: I am taking a break

masterLandsFourthQuests.questThirteen: Assemble the sentence: I finish work today at 16:00

masterLandsFifthQuests.title: Responsibilities and Tasks
masterLandsFifthQuests.description: Learning to explain tasks, ask for an example, and report readiness.
masterLandsFifthQuests.questFourteen: Assemble the question: Can you explain this?

masterLandsSixthQuests.title: Tools and Warehouse
masterLandsSixthQuests.description: Words for warehouse work. Equipment, cargo, warehouse.

masterLandsSeventhQuests.title: Time and Punctuality
masterLandsSeventhQuests.description: Learning to arrange a time, be on time, and report arrival.
masterLandsSeventhQuests.questFour: Assemble: I always come on time
masterLandsSeventhQuests.questSix: Fill in the word (tomorrow): Wir sehen uns ___ .
masterLandsSeventhQuests.questTen: Fill in the word (late): Entschuldigung, ich bin zu ___ .

masterLandsEightQuests.title: Loading and Weights
masterLandsEightQuests.description: Physical work: loading, unloading, weights, and assistance.

masterLandsEightQuests.questFifteen: Assemble the sentence: This package is empty

masterLandsNineQuests.title: Salary and Rest
masterLandsNineQuests.description: Dealing with the essentials: salary, sick leave, and days off.
masterLandsNineQuests.questThree: How to say: End of the workday (Feierabend)
masterLandsNineQuests.questSix: Fill in the word (sick): Ich bin heute ___ .
masterLandsNineQuests.questSeven: Assemble the sentence: I need to see a doctor
masterLandsNineQuests.questTwelve: Assemble the sentence: I need a vacation
masterLandsNineQuests.questThirteen: Assemble the sentence: Will I get paid today?

masterLandsTenQuests.title: Tools and the Site
masterLandsTenQuests.description: Tools and cleaning at the workplace.
masterLandsTenQuests.questSix: Fill in the word (ladder): Die ___ ist kaputt.
masterLandsTenQuests.questTen: Fill in the word (shovel): Ich brauche eine ___ .
masterLandsTenQuests.questEleven: How to say: To clean/tidy up
masterLandsTenQuests.questTwelve: Assemble: We need to clean up here

masterLandsElevenQuests.title: Physical Labor
masterLandsElevenQuests.description: Work, searching for tools, and safety regulations
masterLandsElevenQuests.questFour: Assemble the sentence: Help me carry this
masterLandsElevenQuests.questFive: How to say: To search/look for

masterLandsElevenQuests.questFourteen: Assemble the sentence: There is a danger to life here

masterLandsTwelveQuests.title: Sizes and Counting
masterLandsTwelveQuests.description: Here we will learn to talk about: sizes, counting, and the order of execution
masterLandsTwelveQuests.questFour: Assemble: This is three meters long
masterLandsTwelveQuests.questTen: Fill in the word (first): ___ machen wir das.
masterLandsTwelveQuests.questThirteen: Assemble the sentence: We only need half

masterLandsThirteenQuests.title: Salary and Contract
masterLandsThirteenQuests.description: About money and documents: reviewing vocabulary regarding salary and contract
masterLandsThirteenQuests.questSeven: Assemble the sentence: I have a contract
masterLandsThirteenQuests.questNine: How to say: I am signing the contract
masterLandsThirteenQuests.questTen: Fill in the word (sign): Ich ___ den Vertrag.
masterLandsThirteenQuests.questThirteen: Assemble the sentence: I pay taxes in Germany

masterLandsFourteenQuests.title: Sick Leave and Health
masterLandsFourteenQuests.description: How to report an illness, go to the doctor, and provide a medical certificate
masterLandsFourteenQuests.questThree: How to say: To report sick (take sick leave)
masterLandsFourteenQuests.questFour: Assemble the sentence: I must go to the doctor
masterLandsFourteenQuests.questFive: Assemble the sentence: I am taking sick leave today
masterLandsFourteenQuests.questSix: How to say: I cannot work
masterLandsFourteenQuests.questSeven: How to say: I cannot work
masterLandsFourteenQuests.questEight: Assemble the sentence: Here is my sick leave certificate

masterLandsFourteenQuests.questTwelve: How to say: I must inform the employer
masterLandsFourteenQuests.questThirteen: Assemble the sentence: I am on sick leave for three days

eastPlainFirstQuests.title: First Meeting
eastPlainFirstQuests.description: You have just arrived on an unfamiliar continent. First, you need to learn how to greet locals and express gratitude to make your first acquaintance.
eastPlainFirstQuests.goal: Take the first step. Learn words of greeting and gratitude.
eastPlainFirstQuests.questTen: Build the sentence: Hello! How are you? Good, thank you!
eastPlainFirstQuests.questFourteen: How to say 'Very good, thank you'

eastPlainSecondQuests.title: Day Two
eastPlainSecondQuests.description: After the first meeting, you continue talking. The locals share information about themselves: where they are from and what their names are. You learn to ask polite questions.
eastPlainSecondQuests.questOne: How to say: See you tomorrow
eastPlainSecondQuests.questFive: Build the sentence: 'Hello Thomas! How are you? Good, and you? Good, thanks!'
eastPlainSecondQuests.questSix: Build the sentence: 'See you soon, Thomas'
eastPlainSecondQuests.questSeven: Build the sentence: 'Hello! Where are you from?'
eastPlainSecondQuests.questTwelve: How to say 'What is your name?'
eastPlainSecondQuests.questThirteen: Write: Hello! My name is Thomas
eastPlainSecondQuests.questFourteen: How to say 'What is your name?'

eastPlainThirdQuests.title: What do you like to do?
eastPlainThirdQuests.goal: Learn to talk about hobbies and free time
eastPlainThirdQuests.description: New friends invite you to spend time together, suggesting going to the store or supermarket. You learn to talk about your intentions: where you are going and what you are doing.
eastPlainThirdQuests.questTree: How to say: I’m going to the shop
eastPlainThirdQuests.questFive: How to say: Hello! Where are you going? I’m going to the shop
eastPlainThirdQuests.questSeven: How to say: I’m also going to the shop
eastPlainThirdQuests.questEleven: How to say: I’m not going to the supermarket

eastPlainFourthQuests.title: Continue shopping?
eastPlainFourthQuests.description: At the store, you reach the checkout. You learn to use words like queue, price, and payment methods (cash, card).
eastPlainFourthQuests.goal: Learn to use the concepts of queue, price, and payment methods
eastPlainFourthQuests.questTree: Build: I’m standing in the queue

eastPlainFifthQuests.title: What do you eat?
eastPlainFifthQuests.description: After shopping, you decide to have a snack at a café. You learn to order food and drinks (tea, coffee, water), politely ask and inquire about prices.
eastPlainFifthQuests.goal: Learn to talk about food, buying, and ordering
eastPlainFifthQuests.questFive: Build the sentence: I would like coffee, please
eastPlainFifthQuests.questSix: How to ask: How much does it cost?
eastPlainFifthQuests.questSeven: Fill in: Wie ___ kostet das Getränk?
eastPlainFifthQuests.questEight: What does the customer want to buy?
eastPlainFifthQuests.questTen: How to say: Bottle of water
eastPlainFifthQuests.questEleven: How to say 'I like to drink tea'
eastPlainFifthQuests.questTwelve: Fill in the blank: ___ viel kostet Wasser?
eastPlainFifthQuests.questThirteen: Build the sentence: Tea and bread with butter, please

eastPlainSixthQuests.title: What is your job?
eastPlainSixthQuests.description: At the café, you meet new people and talk about work. You learn to name different professions (teacher, doctor, cook) and ask about others’ occupations.
eastPlainSixthQuests.goal: Learn to talk about professions and ask questions
eastPlainSixthQuests.questSix: Build the sentence: What is your profession?
eastPlainSixthQuests.questThirteen: Fill in: I work as a doctor → Ich arbeite als ___
eastPlainSixthQuests.questFourteen: Build the sentence: He works in the hospital

eastPlainSeventhQuests.title: Time and prices
eastPlainSeventhQuests.description: Your day is full: you hear the train departure time and market prices. You practice using numbers, time, and dates in shopping and schedules.
eastPlainSeventhQuests.goal: We continue learning about prices and numbers
eastPlainSeventhQuests.questOne: Choose the correct answer: How much is it?
eastPlainSeventhQuests.questEight: How much does the bread cost?
eastPlainSeventhQuests.questTen: How to say '3rd of May' in German?
eastPlainSeventhQuests.questThirteen: Build a sentence from the given words
eastPlainSeventhQuests.questFourteen: What time does the train arrive?

eastPlainEighthQuests.title: My Family
eastPlainEighthQuests.description: It’s time to tell more about yourself. You share information about your family (mother, father, brother, uncle) and learn to understand stories about others’ families.
eastPlainEighthQuests.goal: Learn to talk about family and ask simple questions
eastPlainEighthQuests.questFourFirstOption: Do you have brothers or sisters?
eastPlainEighthQuests.questFourSecondOption: Do you live with your parents?
eastPlainEighthQuests.questFourAnswer: Do you have brothers or sisters?
eastPlainEighthQuests.questFive: Build the sentence: He is my uncle
eastPlainEighthQuests.questSix: Build the sentence: My mother’s name is Anna
eastPlainEighthQuests.questNine: Read aloud: Er ist mein Sohn
eastPlainEighthQuests.questEleven: Fill in: This is my grandmother → Das ist meine ___
eastPlainEighthQuests.questTwelve: Build the sentence: She is my mother

eastPlainNinthQuests.title: My Day
eastPlainNinthQuests.description: You are asked about your daily routine. You learn to talk about when you wake up, have breakfast, work, and have free time.
eastPlainNinthQuests.goal: Learn to describe your day and understand others’ routines
eastPlainNinthQuests.questOneSecondOption: I go to bed at 6 o’clock
eastPlainNinthQuests.questFour: Build the sentence: In the morning I drink water
eastPlainNinthQuests.questSeven: Build the sentence: I get up at 7 o’clock
eastPlainNinthQuests.questElevenThirdOption: I have lunch in the evening
eastPlainNinthQuests.questFourteen: When does Maria go to work?

eastPlainTenthQuests.title: Where do you live?
eastPlainTenthQuests.description: Final stage: you talk about your home (apartment, house) and where you come from, using prepositions of place.
eastPlainTenthQuests.goal: Describe again where you live and where you come from
eastPlainTenthQuests.questTreeFirstOption: He lives in the city center
eastPlainTenthQuests.questTreeAnswer: He lives in the city center

questLakeRuinsFirstQuests.title: Weather and Clothing
questLakeRuinsFirstQuests.description: You are going on a trip to the lake. Before leaving, you need to check the weather: sunny, rainy, or cold. You learn to ask about the weather and decide what to wear.
questLakeRuinsFirstQuests.goal: Understand basic weather words and form short phrases
questLakeRuinsFirstQuests.questNine: Do you need a jacket today?
questLakeRuinsFirstQuests.questFifteen: What's the weather in the evening?

questLakeRuinsSecondQuests.title: Seasons and Temperature
questLakeRuinsSecondQuests.description: At the lake, you discuss the changing seasons (summer, winter) and temperature in degrees. You learn to talk about when it’s hot and when it’s cold.
questLakeRuinsSecondQuests.goal: Learn to talk about seasons and weather
questLakeRuinsSecondQuests.questEleven: Fill in the gap: Im ___ ist es warm.
questLakeRuinsSecondQuests.questFifteen: What's the weather like in autumn?

questLakeRuinsThirdQuests.title: Forecast and Plans
questLakeRuinsThirdQuests.description: You check the weather forecast for today/tomorrow and plan the weekend. You learn to connect the forecast with simple plans (stay home, go to the mountains).
questLakeRuinsThirdQuests.goal: Talk about weather (without degrees) and link it to simple plans
questLakeRuinsThirdQuests.questNine: What are we doing on the weekend?

questLakeRuinsFourthQuests.title: Forecast and Bad Weather
questLakeRuinsFourthQuests.description: The weather worsens. You face a storm, thunder, lightning, and wind. You learn to describe strong weather conditions and say what you need to take (raincoat, boots).
questLakeRuinsFourthQuests.goal: Expand bad weather vocabulary and make simple sentences
questLakeRuinsFourthQuests.questNine: What do they advise to take?

questLakeRuinsFifthQuests.title: Fog, Hail and Forecast
questLakeRuinsFifthQuests.description: The bad weather continues: fog, hail, thunderstorm. The road becomes slippery. You learn to understand a detailed forecast (Vorhersage) and talk about gloves.
questLakeRuinsFifthQuests.goal: Talk about bad weather and forecasts

questLakeRuinsSixthQuests.title: Heat and Sun
questLakeRuinsSixthQuests.description: The weather changes suddenly — it’s hot and sunny. You learn to talk about shade, sun protection (cream, glasses) and describe humid weather.
questLakeRuinsSixthQuests.goal: Talk about heat and sun protection
questLakeRuinsSixthQuests.questSeven: What do they recommend to use?

questLakeRuinsSeventhQuests.title: Forecast and Weather Changes
questLakeRuinsSeventhQuests.description: You discuss how the weather changes: clear, variable, temperature rises/falls during the day or night. You learn to understand temperature ranges.
questLakeRuinsSeventhQuests.goal: Understand and talk about the weather forecast
questLakeRuinsSeventhQuests.questNine: What’s the weather like during the day?

questLakeRuinsEighthQuests.title: Combine: Season, Weather, Degrees, Clothes
questLakeRuinsEighthQuests.description: You bring together all your weather knowledge: discuss which clothes fit windy and cold weather, and understand forecasts with temperatures by season.
questLakeRuinsEighthQuests.questOne: Translate: Im Sommer ist es heiß.
questLakeRuinsEighthQuests.questSixSecondOption: Taking an umbrella and a hat
questLakeRuinsEighthQuests.questThirteen: What clothes are suitable? Es ist kalt und windig.

questLakeRuinsNinthQuests.title: Planning the Day by Forecast
questLakeRuinsNinthQuests.description: You compare the morning/day/evening forecasts in different cities and decide if a jacket is needed. You learn to plan your day based on detailed weather forecasts.
questLakeRuinsNinthQuests.goal: Combine known words about weather and clothing
questLakeRuinsNinthQuests.questOne: Translate: Wie ist das Wetter heute in Berlin?
questLakeRuinsNinthQuests.questOneFirstOption: What’s the weather like in Berlin today?
questLakeRuinsNinthQuests.questOneSecondOption: When is the train to Berlin?
questLakeRuinsNinthQuests.questTwo: When is it sunny in Berlin?
questLakeRuinsNinthQuests.questEightFirstOption: It stays warm during the day
questLakeRuinsNinthQuests.questEightSecondOption: It gets cold in the morning
questLakeRuinsNinthQuests.questNine: How many degrees during the day in Munich?
questLakeRuinsNinthQuests.questThirteen: Is a jacket needed during the day?

questLakeRuinsTenthQuests.title: Weekend Plans
questLakeRuinsTenthQuests.description: You look at the full weekend forecast, compare temperatures and decide where to go (park, museum), combining all your knowledge.
questLakeRuinsTenthQuests.questTwo: When is it warmer in Berlin?
questLakeRuinsTenthQuests.questFive: Translate: Die Temperatur sinkt am Abend.
questLakeRuinsTenthQuests.questFiveFirstOption: The temperature drops in the evening
questLakeRuinsTenthQuests.questFiveSecondOption: The temperature rises in the evening
questLakeRuinsTenthQuests.questFiveThirdOption: The temperature stays the same in the evening
questLakeRuinsTenthQuests.questSix: What happens to the temperature in the evening?

questsNorthCliffsFirstQuests.title: Booking and Check-in
questsNorthCliffsFirstQuests.description: You arrive at a hotel on the Northern Cliffs. You need to book and check in at the reception, asking for a single/double room and the price.

questsNorthCliffsSecondQuests.title: Questions at the Hotel Reception
questsNorthCliffsSecondQuests.description: You ask questions at the reception: about breakfast, Wi-Fi, and the elevator. You also ask for the bill and pay for the room.
questsNorthCliffsSecondQuests.questNine: How much is a double room?
questsNorthCliffsSecondQuests.questThirteen: How much is a late check-out?

questsNorthCliffsThirdQuests.title: Problems in the Room
questsNorthCliffsThirdQuests.description: There are issues in the room. You need to report a heating problem and ask for extra items or cleaning.
questsNorthCliffsThirdQuests.goal: asking for help with household issues

questsNorthCliffsFourthQuests.title: Services and Schedules
questsNorthCliffsFourthQuests.description: You are getting fully comfortable in the hotel. Now you need to find out the breakfast hours and make sure you know where the elevator and stairs are. You also ask if the parking is paid.

questsNorthCliffsFifthQuests.title: Check-out and Airport
questsNorthCliffsFifthQuests.description: It’s departure day. You need to pay the bill (cash/card), get the receipt, and return the key card. You ask to call a taxi to the airport and check if you can leave your luggage for storage.
questsNorthCliffsFifthQuests.goal: Taxi/airport, payment (cash/card), receipt/invoice, late check-out, leaving luggage, announcements
questsNorthCliffsFifthQuests.questSeven: Build a question about late check-out
questsNorthCliffsFifthQuests.questNine: Where do you get the bill/receipt?
questsNorthCliffsFifthQuests.questEleven: Build the question: Where is the airport?
questsNorthCliffsFifthQuests.questFourteen: Build the phrase: This is my luggage

questsNorthCliffsSixthQuests.title: Trip and Hotel — Mini Dialogues
questsNorthCliffsSixthQuests.description: You practice using all learned hotel phrases: reservation, check-in, requests (towels), paying the bill, and going to the airport.
questsNorthCliffsSixthQuests.goal: Combine learned phrases: reservation, quiet room, breakfast times, elevator, bill/receipt, pay (cash/card), taxi/airport
questsNorthCliffsSixthQuests.questOne: For how many nights is the reservation?

questsNorthCliffsSeventhQuests.title: Hotel Dialogues
questsNorthCliffsSeventhQuests.description: Short, quick dialogues where you must respond instantly: room availability, Wi-Fi password, room problems, taxi request, and payment.
questsNorthCliffsSeventhQuests.goal: Quickly understand and build typical hotel phrases
questsNorthCliffsSeventhQuests.questTwo: Build the question: 'Is the room available?'
questsNorthCliffsSeventhQuests.questFour: Build an answer about the elevator
questsNorthCliffsSeventhQuests.questFive: Where can you get the Wi-Fi password?
questsNorthCliffsSeventhQuests.questSeven: Build the phrase: 'There is a problem in the room'
questsNorthCliffsSeventhQuests.questEightSecondOption: A blanket and a remote control
questsNorthCliffsSeventhQuests.questEleven: What time does the taxi arrive?
questsNorthCliffsSeventhQuests.questThirteen: Build the question: Where are the stairs?

questsNorthCliffsEighthQuests.title: Trip — City Transport
questsNorthCliffsEighthQuests.description: You go out into the city and decide to use public transport. You look for a stop or station, ask where the bus or subway goes, and buy a ticket.
questsNorthCliffsEighthQuests.goal: Use basic phrases in public transportation
questsNorthCliffsEighthQuests.questThird: Build the question: Where does this bus go?
questsNorthCliffsEighthQuests.questEight: How to say: 'Where is the station?'
questsNorthCliffsEighthQuests.questTen: How to say to the driver: 'To the station, please'
questsNorthCliffsEighthQuests.questEleven: Build: Ticket for the subway, please
questsNorthCliffsEighthQuests.questThirteen: What time does the subway arrive?
questsNorthCliffsEighthQuests.questFourteen: Build the question: Where is the stop?

questsNorthCliffsNinthQuests.title: Train — Station and Platforms
questsNorthCliffsNinthQuests.description: You are at the station. You learn to read the schedule: platform, departure, and arrival. You learn to ask about delays and transfers.
questsNorthCliffsNinthQuests.goal: Understand announcements and ask questions at the station
questsNorthCliffsNinthQuests.questFive: Build: The train departs from platform 4
questsNorthCliffsNinthQuests.questSeven: Build the question: From which platform does the train leave?
questsNorthCliffsNinthQuests.questNine: From which platform does the train depart?
questsNorthCliffsNinthQuests.questThirteen: How long is the train delayed?

questsNorthCliffsTenthQuests.title: Trip — Airport and Flight
questsNorthCliffsTenthQuests.description: You are at the airport. You go through check-in and security control, look for your boarding gate, and find where to collect your luggage.
questsNorthCliffsTenthQuests.questFive: Build the question: 'Where is gate A12?'
questsNorthCliffsTenthQuests.questFifteen: Which gate should you go to?

questsNorthCliffsEleventhQuests.title: Trip — final practice
questsNorthCliffsEleventhQuests.description: You combine all your travel knowledge: hotel, transport, train station, airport. You reinforce the skills needed for independent travel.
questsNorthCliffsEleventhQuests.questTwo: Make a request at the counter:
questsNorthCliffsEleventhQuests.questFive: How long is the train delayed?
questsNorthCliffsEleventhQuests.questSix: Make a question at the airport:
questsNorthCliffsEleventhQuests.questFifteen: To which gate should I go?

questsNorthCliffsTwelfthQuests.title: Trip — more practice
questsNorthCliffsTwelfthQuests.description: The last set of mini-situations to feel confident using travel, booking, transport, and airport phrases.
questsNorthCliffsTwelfthQuests.questTwo: Make a phrase about the room:
questsNorthCliffsTwelfthQuests.questFive: From which platform does the train depart?
questsNorthCliffsTwelfthQuests.questSix: Make a question at the station:
questsNorthCliffsTwelfthQuests.questTen: Make a complaint in the room:
questsNorthCliffsTwelfthQuests.questThirteen: When is the next bus after 13:10?
questsNorthCliffsTwelfthQuests.questFourteen: Make a sign for the door:

questsStoneReachFirstQuests.title: Welcome to the Village
questsStoneReachFirstQuests.description: You enter the first house in Grammeldorf. You need to name basic items in the room (table, lamp, book) and learn to use simple adjectives (small, big, new, old).
questsStoneReachFirstQuests.goal: Get to know basic objects and simple phrases.
questsStoneReachFirstQuests.questSix: Build the sentence: This is a lamp
questsStoneReachFirstQuests.questSeven: How to say: This is a book
questsStoneReachFirstQuests.questEight: How to say: This is a lamp
questsStoneReachFirstQuests.questNine: Build the sentence: This is a table
questsStoneReachFirstQuests.questFifteen: Build the sentence: This is a window

questsStoneReachSecondQuests.title: In the Herbalist’s House
questsStoneReachSecondQuests.description: You are visiting an old herbalist. To stay, you must correctly name the rooms (kitchen, bedroom, bathroom) and basic furniture (bed, chair).
questsStoneReachSecondQuests.goal: Learn to name rooms and objects in the house.
questsStoneReachSecondQuests.questSix: Choose the correct phrase: This is a table
questsStoneReachSecondQuests.questSeven: Choose the correct phrase: This is a lamp
questsStoneReachSecondQuests.questNine: Choose the correct phrase: This is a bedroom
questsStoneReachSecondQuests.questTwelve: Choose the correct phrase: This is a bathroom
questsStoneReachSecondQuests.questThirteen: Choose the correct phrase: This is a window
questsStoneReachSecondQuests.questFourteen: Choose the correct phrase: This is a kitchen

questsStoneReachThirdQuests.title: Colors Around
questsStoneReachThirdQuests.description: You start describing the objects around you using colors (red, blue, green, yellow).
questsStoneReachThirdQuests.goal: Learn to recognize phrases with colors and objects.
questsStoneReachThirdQuests.questOne: What does 'Die Lampe ist rot' mean?
questsStoneReachThirdQuests.questThirteen: What does 'Der Teppich ist grün' mean?

questsStoneReachFourthQuests.title: How Many Are There?
questsStoneReachFourthQuests.description: A carpenter asks you to help him count his inventory (books, chairs).
questsStoneReachFourthQuests.goal: Understand phrases with numbers and objects: es gibt / ich habe / wie viele.

questsStoneReachFifthQuests.title: In the Kitchen
questsStoneReachFifthQuests.description: In the kitchen you learn the names of dishes (fork, spoon, plate, glass) and simple combinations with articles and adjectives.
questsStoneReachFifthQuests.goal: Learn kitchen items and simple phrases like Das ist / ein, eine, der, die, das.
questsStoneReachFifthQuests.questThird: How to say: This is a fork
questsStoneReachFifthQuests.questSeven: How to say: This is a spoon
questsStoneReachFifthQuests.questEleven: How to say: This is a plate

questsStoneReachSixthQuests.title: In the Living Room
questsStoneReachSixthQuests.description: You describe the living room: sofa, armchair, carpet, TV. You make simple descriptive sentences.
questsStoneReachSixthQuests.goal: Name objects in the living room and make simple Das ist ... sentences.
questsStoneReachSixthQuests.questFour: Build the sentence: This is a sofa
questsStoneReachSixthQuests.questFifteen: How to say: This is a carpet

questsStoneReachSeventhQuests.title: In the Bathroom
questsStoneReachSeventhQuests.description: You learn bathroom vocabulary: shower, bathtub, sink, towel. You learn to talk about quantity (two towels) and quality (clean towel).
questsStoneReachSeventhQuests.goal: Name bathroom items and simple phrases with numbers and adjectives.
questsStoneReachSeventhQuests.questFive: Build the sentence: This is a shower

questsStoneReachEighthQuests.title: In the Bedroom
questsStoneReachEighthQuests.description: You describe the bedroom: bed, pillow, blanket, wardrobe, nightstand. You make phrases with adjectives (soft pillow).
questsStoneReachEighthQuests.goal: Name bedroom items and make simple sentences Das ist / ein, eine, der, die, das.
questsStoneReachEighthQuests.questFour: Build the sentence: This is a bed
questsStoneReachEighthQuests.questThirteen: Build the sentence: This is a mirror

questsStoneReachNinthQuests.title: In the Hallway
questsStoneReachNinthQuests.description: At the entrance, you learn words related to going out: door, key, jacket, coat, hat, shoes. You practice short descriptions of these items.
questsStoneReachNinthQuests.goal: Name hallway items and make short phrases with articles and numbers.
questsStoneReachNinthQuests.questFour: Build the sentence: This is a jacket
questsStoneReachNinthQuests.questSeven: Build the sentence: Two new shoes
questsStoneReachNinthQuests.questThirteen: Build the sentence: This is a door

questsStoneReachTenthQuests.title: In the Garden
questsStoneReachTenthQuests.description: You go outside into the garden and learn the names of plants and tools (tree, flower, watering can, shovel) and talk about their colors and quantities.
questsStoneReachTenthQuests.goal: Name garden objects and make simple combinations with colors and numbers.

questsStormCoastsFirstQuests.title: Holidays — Invitation and Time
questsStormCoastsFirstQuests.description: You’re invited to a local celebration. You learn to understand the invitation, find the address and the date.
questsStormCoastsFirstQuests.goal: Understand and use basic phrases about invitations and date/time: Einladung, feiern, Party, am Samstag, um 18 Uhr, Adresse, kommen/nicht kommen
questsStormCoastsFirstQuests.questFive: Build the phrase: “I celebrate on Saturday”
questsStormCoastsFirstQuests.questSix: Choose the correct sentence about the party date
questsStormCoastsFirstQuests.questSeven: Build the phrase: “The party starts at 6 p.m.”
questsStormCoastsFirstQuests.questFourteen: Build a goodbye phrase like “See you”

questsStormCoastsSecondQuests.title: Holidays — Congratulations and Gifts
questsStormCoastsSecondQuests.description: You prepare for the celebration: choose a gift, flowers and a greeting card. You learn how to congratulate and give wishes.
questsStormCoastsSecondQuests.goal: gratulieren, Geschenk, schenken, Blumen, Glückwunschkarte, verpacken; formulas for congratulations
questsStormCoastsSecondQuests.questEleven: Fill the gap: Ich ___ dir (I congratulate you)
questsStormCoastsSecondQuests.questFourteen: Build a full birthday congratulation formula

questsStormCoastsThirdQuests.title: Holidays — Organization
questsStormCoastsThirdQuests.description: You help organize the party: discuss the plan, guest list, food (cake) and music. You ask who will bring what (mitbringen).
questsStormCoastsThirdQuests.goal: planen, Gäste, Dekoration, Kuchen, Musik, mitbringen, einkaufen, vorbereiten, Ort; making arrangements
questsStormCoastsThirdQuests.questFive: Build: We celebrate in the park
questsStormCoastsThirdQuests.questSix: Fill the gap in the question: ___ du etwas mit?
questsStormCoastsThirdQuests.questNine: Where is the party taking place?
questsStormCoastsThirdQuests.questTwelve: Build the question: Who brings the music?

questsStormCoastsFourQuests.title: Holidays — Throughout the Year
questsStormCoastsFourQuests.description: You get to know the main holidays (Christmas, Easter, New Year), traditions, and learn when public holidays are. You practice simple greetings.
questsStormCoastsFourQuests.goal: Weihnachten, Ostern, Silvester, Neujahr, der Feiertag, Tradition, frei haben; simple date facts
questsStormCoastsFourQuests.questFive: Build: We have a day off
questsStormCoastsFourQuests.questEleven: Build: We celebrate with the family
questsStormCoastsFourQuests.questTwelve: Build: At Easter we eat eggs
questsStormCoastsFourQuests.questThirteen: When do many families celebrate Christmas?

questsStormCoastsFifthQuests.title: Holidays — Mini Dialogues and Practice
questsStormCoastsFifthQuests.description: You practice all holiday phrases: invitation, confirmation/refusal, congratulations, asking for help, discussing date and time.
questsStormCoastsFifthQuests.goal: Combine: Einladung, feiern, Zeit/Ort, gratulieren, Geschenk, Gäste, Dekoration, Musik, Silvester/Weihnachten/Ostern/Neujahr, Feiertag, frei haben
questsStormCoastsFifthQuests.questThird: Fill in the blank (unfortunately): Ich kann ___ nicht

questsStormCoastsFifthQuests.questFive: Build a request for help with the music
questsStormCoastsFifthQuests.questEight: Build: We are making a cake
questsStormCoastsFifthQuests.questNine: Fill in the blank (drinks): Ich kaufe ___
questsStormCoastsFifthQuests.questTwelve: Впиши пропуск (семьей): Wir feiern mit der ___
questsStormCoastsFifthQuests.questFourteen: Build: I will bring a salad

questsStormCoastsSixQuests.title: Holidays — Date, Time, and Confirmation
questsStormCoastsSixQuests.description: In-depth work with invitations: date, time, address. Learn to politely confirm or decline and ask if you can come with someone.
questsStormCoastsSixQuests.goal: Datum, Uhrzeit, am/um, kommen/nicht kommen, vielleicht, mitbringen, Bescheid sagen
questsStormCoastsSixQuests.questThird: Build: The party is on April 3

questsStormCoastsSixQuests.questNine: By which day do you need to reply?
questsStormCoastsSixQuests.questTwelve: Build: I will send the invitation by e-mail

questsStormCoastsSevenQuests.title: Holidays — Food and Drinks
questsStormCoastsSevenQuests.description: You help organize the table: discuss food and drinks (juice, lemonade), ask for vegetarian options, ask with/without sugar/alcohol, and arrange who brings what.
questsStormCoastsSevenQuests.goal: Getränke, Snacks, Saft, Wasser, Limonade, ohne/mit, vegetarisch, zwei Flaschen, eine Torte, mitbringen/verteilen

questsStormCoastsEighthQuests.title: Holidays — Dress Code and Program
questsStormCoastsEighthQuests.description: You learn about the dress code (smart/casual), the program (dancing, games, toasts), and how to ask to turn the music down/up.
questsStormCoastsEighthQuests.goal: Dresscode, schick/locker, das Kostüm, tanzen, spielen, Fotos machen, Programm, beginnt/endet um …, Musik leiser/lauter

questsStormCoastsEighthQuests.questEight: How to suggest taking a photo?
questsStormCoastsEighthQuests.questFourteen: How to say: “The dress code is casual”

questsStormCoastsNinthQuests.title: Holidays — Family Events
questsStormCoastsNinthQuests.description: You learn vocabulary for family celebrations: wedding, anniversary, baptism. You practice special congratulations and learn where the celebration takes place (restaurant/church).
questsStormCoastsNinthQuests.goal: die Hochzeit, das Jubiläum, die Taufe, gratulieren zu, zur Hochzeit/Zum Jubiläum, schick, Geschenk, Feier im Restaurant/Kirche

questsStormCoastsTenthQuests.description: A final comprehensive task combining invitations, food, congratulations, and family celebrations.

questsStormCoastsTenthQuests.questNine: Build: We are taking a photo

questsWindValleyFifthQuests.title: Cafés and Leisure
questsWindValleyFifthQuests.description: You get to know places for leisure: restaurant, café, hotel, museum.

questsWindValleyNinthQuests.description: You learn the names of important city services: post office, police, hospital, school.

questsWindValleyNinthQuests.questFifteenFirstOption: The post office is on the left
questsWindValleyNinthQuests.questFifteenSecondOption: The post office is on the right

idioms.title: Idioms and Phrases
idioms.subTitle: Understanding real language through fixed expressions
idioms.isFrazeologizm: — a fixed combination of words whose meaning is not always clear from the individual words.
idioms.isIdiom: — a type of phraseologism whose meaning cannot be understood literally. Like 'speaking in riddles' — it means 'speaking unclearly', not about riddles.
idioms.explainFirstFrazeoligizm: to have a good head on one's shoulders
idioms.explainSecondIdiom: — 'to tie a bear to someone' → 'to pull someone’s leg'.
